about.intro: Daiyuzan Hokyoji is a Soto Zen Buddhist temple founded in 747 CE. Located at the foot of Mt. Fuji and Mt. Kintoki in Oyama-cho, Shizuoka, the temple is beloved by the local community as the 'Takenoshita Jizo-san'. The mountain name 'Daiyuzan' embodies the spirit of Soto Zen, and the temple has continued to receive the prayers of worshippers with Enmei Jizo Bosatsu as its main deity.

honzon.name: Main Deity: Enmei Jizo Bosatsu (Statue)
honzon.tradition: Said to be carved by Prince Shotoku (current statue is from the Kamakura period)
honzon.desc: The Enmei Jizo-son is said to have been carved by Prince Shotoku, and is revered for its many miraculous benefits. Worshippers travel from far and wide for the Hatsu-Jizo and Grand Festival ceremonies. Known affectionately as the 'Takenoshita Jizo-san', it is also recognized today as a sacred power spot.

data.name: Wooden Enmei Jizo Bosatsu Seated Statue
data.designation: Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property (March 19, 1985)
data.tradition: Said to be carved by Prince Shotoku

hatsujizo.content: Daihannya Sutra reading, ancestor memorial service, safety prayer
hatsujizo.comment: The first Jizo memorial day of the new year. A traditional gathering of devoted worshippers.

taisai.content: Drum memorial, Daihannya prayer, sumo dedication in the temple grounds
taisai.comment: The summer grand festival, drawing worshippers from far away.

koyo.content: Bamboo lantern illumination and autumn foliage light-up
koyo.cosponsor: Hokyoji, Takenoshitamiya Shrine, Oyama Tourism Association
koyo.comment: A magical night as the autumn foliage along the approach is illuminated by handmade bamboo lanterns.

practice.copySub: Play in zazen, find space in everyday life.
practice.scheduleNote: Closed in January–February / Schedule announced on Instagram
practice.dress: Please wear comfortable clothing (avoid short skirts with exposed legs)
practice.donation: Please make a free-will offering (dana) of your choice
practice.private: Please send a DM to Hokyoji's Instagram

access.train: 7-min walk from JR Gotemba Line 'Ashigara Station' (approx. 500–600m)
access.car: Approx. 15 min from Gotemba IC on the Tomei Expressway
access.parking: Approx. 5 spaces in the upper temple grounds (free)
